segments.integrated_caption: The video opens with a dark, purple-hued title screen displaying 'Ökko PHOENIX' with a fiery animation and the website 'phoenix-animation.com', accompanied by whooshing and dramatic orchestral sounds. This transitions into a fast-paced montage of animated characters, including one with spiky hair holding a fan, another with purple hair looking annoyed, and a character with green hair making a heart gesture, all set to an upbeat pop/rock song with female vocals singing about a 'magic stack of cards' and how 'everything went wrong'. A chaotic scene with a blue car crashing through a wall leads to a glowing '13 CARDS' title. The scene then shifts to a cozy, colorful living room, where the music softens to a gentle, melancholic string melody. A cheerful teal-haired character peeks from behind a door, walks into the room, picks up a blue remote, and turns on a retro TV, which emits electronic beeps and clicks as it displays a pixelated game. The teal-haired character smiles, then sits on a plush pink couch as three other characters—one with yellow hair, one with green hair, and one with spiky purple hair—appear, looking annoyed or unimpressed. The teal-haired character sighs, then a male voice asks, "What you watching?" prompting a female gasp/scream from the teal-haired character, who then looks downcast while the others maintain their stern expressions.

segments.integrated_caption: The scene opens with a close-up of the Green-haired character, who has a mischievous grin and green spiral glasses. Electronic game sounds fill the air as the Teal-haired character, Wadu, cheerfully exclaims, "Time to rock and roll!" while turning on the Retro TV with the Blue remote. The TV displays a pixelated game featuring a robot and a flower-like object, but Wadu soon appears dejected, letting out a frustrated "Ah!" The Purple-haired character, with a stern, unimpressed look, stands behind the Pink couch, holding the Blue remote, and firmly declares, "No! We're not watching this crap today!" Wadu protests, "But it's my turn today!" to which the Purple-haired character retorts, "Wadu, we never took turns!" A brief struggle for the remote ensues, accompanied by grunts and TV static. The Green-haired character then stands with a smug expression, raising a hand, taunting the Yellow-haired character, Spade, with "Oh, Spade, give it to me. You never watch TV anyway." Spade, leaning on the couch with a neutral to slightly annoyed expression, responds with a calm, interested tone, "I am tonight. They're showing the 16th-century Ottoman-Hungarian War on Epoch TV," as dramatic historical music begins. Spade then excitedly urges, "Oh, turn it up!" Wadu, still dejected, groans, "Uh, not more power struggles." The Green-haired character, now sitting on the couch with a smug look, sarcastically adds, "We watch TV to become stupider, not smarter!" Spade defends his choice, "There are conky buns in this!" The Purple-haired character, still stern, warns, "Spade, don't give him the remote! Or he'll put on his soap operas for housewives!" Spade clarifies, "They're not just for housewives, but also for divorced men in their thirties." The scene shifts to the kitchen area, where a new Brown-haired character, Fyodor, wearing a yellow shirt with a black collar and blue pants, opens the refrigerator, revealing a mostly empty fridge. He looks disappointed and then angry, hesitantly muttering "Uh..." before suggesting, "What about a cooking show?" The scene returns to the living room, where the Purple-haired character, still holding the Blue remote, sternly tells Fyodor, "Just keep your calm, Fyodor. You can watch it later!" The overall atmosphere is one of mild tension and disappointment, with the characters' expressions and body language reflecting their emotions in the brightly colored, cozy yet slightly chaotic living room.

segments.integrated_caption: The scene opens in a brightly colored living room where the Purple-haired character, holding the Blue remote, stands sternly behind the Pink couch. The Teal-haired character sits dejectedly, while the Yellow-haired character leans on the couch with a neutral expression. The Brown-haired character, initially heard excitedly exclaiming, "Oh yeah! My favorite cartoons are on today!", then struggles as the Purple-haired character uses the Blue remote to pour a dark liquid into his mug. The Brown-haired character reacts with shock and disgust, letting out a despairing "Nooooo!" as he finds the refrigerator empty, leading to a frustrated expression. He continues to express distress, then appears angry, holding a piece of paper, possibly a note or receipt, while the Purple-haired character watches with a stern, dismissive look, uttering a dry "Oops." The Brown-haired character, still distressed, covers his face in despair, letting out a cartoonish scream of "Wah-hoo!" as the Purple-haired character dismissively states, "Who cares? It's just comics. You'll make more." The scene shifts to a framed drawing titled 'Thirteen Cards,' which the Brown-haired character gestures toward, becoming agitated and questioning, "More? More?" The Green-haired character enters, smirking and raising a hand, later declaring energetically, "Need I remind you that I'm here to get stupider!" The Yellow-haired character looks on with concern as the Brown-haired character, frustrated, sighs, "Just... why bother?" and then challenges the Purple-haired character, "You think making comics is easy?" to which the Purple-haired character dryly retorts, "Is it not?" The Purple-haired character then adopts a lecturing tone, providing a condescending historical account of comic books as "a primitive form of entertainment for the peasants." The Teal-haired character is heard lamenting, "I just wanted to watch some cartoons," prompting the Brown-haired character to passionately rally, "Exactly! Do you hear what our spongy friend said? Cartoons are cool! Comics are..." The Purple-haired character, still stern, challenges, "So you're saying you'll do better than me?" The Brown-haired character, now smiling mischievously and holding a piece of paper and a backpack, reveals a crucial detail: "We're literally your clones. You've literally called us up for this," suggesting a shift in mood or plan, as the Purple-haired character begins to respond, "Well, if it's that..." before being cut off.

segments.integrated_caption: The video opens with a close-up of a blank sheet of paper surrounded by scattered colored pencils on a pink surface, setting a creative tone. A male voice, later identified as the Green-haired character, declares, "Then all of you will make a comic each! The best one, in my opinion, will choose what we're gonna watch," followed by his own laughter and a mischievous "Who needs a remote!" as a pouring sound accompanies a dark liquid being dispensed. He then adds, "Good luck!" and "Oh, well that'll keep him busy till the evening." The scene transitions to the Living room, where the Purple-haired character stands sternly behind the Pink couch, holding the Blue remote, while the Yellow-haired character leans on the couch with a neutral expression and the Teal-haired character sits dejectedly. The Green-haired character, standing with a smug look, raises a hand as if making a point. Footsteps and paper rustling are heard as the Brown-haired character enters, reacting with a gasp of shock to the dark liquid poured from the Blue remote into a mug. Upbeat, whimsical background music begins. The Brown-haired character then excitedly exclaims, "I want this one and this one! Oh, this one's pretty," before finding an empty refrigerator and holding a paper, asking with frustration, "Uh, where's all the paper?" The Yellow-haired character chimes in, "You can have toilet paper!", followed by the Teal-haired character's exclamation, "Water!" The Brown-haired character then declares, "I thought of a masterpiece! And for that, I need a lot of paper!" The Green-haired character interjects, "You're not gonna need these anymore!" accompanied by a tearing sound. The Teal-haired character then states, "Oh, I'll do something much, much better," and is later seen in the kitchen, looking distressed while holding a purple pencil, pondering, "Hmm, comic books, comic books. What kinds are there?" He considers, "Ah, there's some about safety, like in school," but dismisses it as "No, too boring," and similarly rejects "caricatures like in Punch magazine?" as "No, too vulgar." The group gathers around a table, with the Green-haired character laughing and pointing playfully at the Yellow-haired character, who looks distressed. The Brown-haired character then holds up a sign reading 'CARTOONS ARE COOL,' adding a mischievous tone. The video concludes with a series of illustrated panels: one showing construction site safety rules with the characters in hard hats, and another depicting a chaotic, surreal scene with exaggerated expressions and a speech bubble asking, 'Are we now in some alternative universe, where irony is king?' The final frame shows the group in a chaotic living room, with the Brown-haired character holding a phone and the Yellow-haired character crawling on the floor, while the Purple-haired character looks on sternly.

What if Yura doesn't like it?" followed by a high-pitched scream and her exclaiming, "That's such a huge responsibility!" The Brown-haired character later appears in a different room, holding a box of crayons and a ruler, seemingly overwhelmed by creative tasks, as pencil scribbling sounds are heard. He calmly advises, "It's important to break the work into stages. For starters, I must collect more data," but then sighs, "Hmm. This is still not enough," amidst more scribbling. Do you like it?" as she shows a drawing to the Yellow-haired character, who, now holding a mug, appears annoyed and dismissive, responding with a curt, "Hmm, yeah, it's fine." The atmosphere shifts from surreal chaos to domestic tension, highlighting emotional struggles and creative frustration among the characters.

segments.integrated_caption: The video opens with the Brown-haired character, wearing a yellow shirt and blue pants, looking frustrated and clenching his fists in an orange room, as a male voice exclaims, "You're just in time!" The Brown-haired character takes the tablet, and the Yellow-haired character’s expression shifts to surprise. The scene cuts to the Green-haired character, now in a blue shirt with a green spiral, drawing on a comic strip with a mischievous grin. A male voice, likely the Brown-haired character, questions, "Um, what is that?" The Green-haired character, with a proud male voice, declares, "I made an animation!" When asked why it's "only three frames," he shouts defensively, "Do you know how hard it is to make cartoons?!" The Brown-haired character watches with a stern expression. The scene shifts to a doorway where the Brown-haired character, now holding a stack of papers, stands between the Yellow-haired character and a new Pink-haired character, both looking concerned. The Brown-haired character's male voice attempts to calm the situation, saying, "All right, all right, all right." The Green-haired character reappears, waving dismissively, and a male voice, likely his, declares, "Move over. King in the castle." Suddenly, the Brown-haired character’s hand is shown with a pink, pixelated substance, which he drops, causing the papers to scatter. A male voice, likely the Brown-haired character, exclaims, "Oh wow, this is beautiful!" but is immediately challenged by another male voice, likely the Green-haired character, asking, "Did you copy the first result for manga on Google?" The first voice defensively replies, "What? No!" The challenging voice then asks, "The second?" to which the defensive voice reluctantly admits, "Yeah." A dramatic sound effect accompanies the Purple-haired character, now with glowing yellow eyes and a menacing expression, appearing in the room and confronting the Brown-haired character. The scene transitions to a dark, stormy mountain landscape where the Purple-haired character, now in a purple armored suit, stands triumphantly with a sword, as a large, winged creature flies overhead. During this, a male voice asks, "Who's next?" followed by a loud "Whoa!" A deep, authoritative male voice declares, "I think I'll restrict your internet access," and another male voice states, "That's enough. It's our turn." The scene cuts back to the room, where the Purple-haired character, now back in his dark blue shirt, glares at the Brown-haired character, who looks terrified. A deep, dramatic male voice with an echo effect begins a narration, "Get ready to read the most exciting saga about a fearless knight whose heroic exploits are glorified by mankind," only to be interrupted by another male voice stating, "It's kinda generic." The Brown-haired character then holds a ruler and a box of crayons, looking overwhelmed in an office space with bookshelves and a whiteboard covered in drawings. A male voice asks, "What was that?" and another male voice critiques, "I mean, it's corny. It doesn't hook you in. The character's motivation is unfun..." The video ends with the Brown-haired character standing in a gray, overcast cityscape, wearing a blue jacket with a fur-lined hood, looking shocked and distressed.

The Gray-haired character, still unimpressed, responds, "Mmm, impressive. And what are the results?" The Brown-haired character then admits, slightly deflated, "I have found... nothing," prompting a dry "Now I know" from the Gray-haired character. The scene then shows the Brown-haired character placing a paper on a pink table where the Pink-haired character is drawing, who then asks in a child-like voice, "Do you think maybe I could take part?" The Brown-haired character then stands proudly in front of the board, addressing a group that includes the Purple-haired, Teal-haired, Yellow-haired, and Pink-haired characters, who react with varying degrees of shock, amusement, and concern. A gasp is heard, followed by the Gray-haired character, now sounding more positive, expressing pride, "I'm proud of you guys," and joking about them not giving up or turning the place upside down, before announcing, "you all actually did great! And so, the rightful winner is the comic by Spade and uh..." He then excitedly exclaims, "I think we have a winner!" The Green-haired character appears behind them, holding a comic strip and smiling mischievously, as the group gathers closely. Various exclamations of surprise and amazement are heard, including "What the...", "Oh!", "It's amazing!", and "What a story! And all on one page." One character questions, "Is this loss?", while another exclaims, "It's beautiful!" The Green-haired character waves dismissively, suggesting a playful or sarcastic conclusion to the investigation, as the atmosphere shifts from tense to chaotic and humorous, with exaggerated expressions and dynamic interactions among the characters.

segments.integrated_caption: The video opens with a close-up of a group of animated characters in a dimly lit room, displaying varied expressions of concern and surprise, as an excited male voice exclaims, "Oh! We have to show it to the world!" This is met with a collective, surprised "What?!" and gasps. The scene quickly shifts to a wider shot of a living room where a purple-haired character with spiky hair and glowing yellow eyes stands menacingly, while others, including a teal-haired character and a yellow-haired character, react with fear. An "Ugh!" sound is heard, followed by an annoyed male voice shouting, "Oh, dang Charlie, watch where you're throwing!" An apologetic female voice, identified as Charlie, replies, "Oh, sorry! I don't even know how I did that!" The focus then moves to a retro-style television screen showing a cartoonish scene of a gray-haired character and a yellow-haired character interacting in a simple, pastel-colored environment. The green-haired character, Charlie, suddenly leaps into the air in excitement, holding a purple object, as the TV displays a cheerful yellow-haired character. An exasperated male voice asks, "How long do we have to watch this for?", to which a deadpan male voice responds, "The whole season," followed by groaning and a sigh. A sudden "Ah!" and a loud thud are heard. On the TV, a calm male voice begins, "Good sleep is the key to...", but is abruptly cut off by an angry male voice yelling, "Just leave!" The scene cuts to a close-up of the purple-haired character, now with a stern expression, followed by a shot of the group on a pink couch, where the brown-haired character smiles confidently while the others look unimpressed. The video then transitions to black-and-white animated credits, accompanied by gentle, melancholic piano music. The credits show a character sleeping in bed, then sitting up and giving a thumbs-up, with text identifying the director, author, and other production roles. The credits continue with stylized line drawings of characters and text listing voice actors, animators, and other crew members, including original and English voice actors, sound engineers, and background artists. The final frames display the production team’s names with accompanying doodles, ending with the 'RESTQUE TEAM' logo and a list of background artists, all set against a black background with white text and minimalistic character sketches.

segments.integrated_caption: The video transitions into a black-and-white animated credit sequence, characterized by a minimalist, hand-drawn aesthetic, all set to a gentle, melancholic piano melody that plays continuously. On the left, a stylized character with spiky hair and a game controller-like object is depicted leaning over a small bird, which has a question mark above it, conveying curiosity or confusion. Simultaneously, on the right, another spiky-haired character sits cross-legged, engrossed in a book, with a thought bubble above them illustrating a serene tropical island scene complete with a hammock and palm tree, suggesting a moment of daydreaming or relaxation. As the soft piano continues, production roles and names appear sequentially in the center, listing 'SELECTING TAKES', 'ASSISTANT CASTING DIRECTOR', 'SOUND EDITING', 'STUDIO ADMINISTRATION', and 'PRODUCERS FROM ÖKKO'. The sequence culminates with a heartfelt message, 'A big thank you to the entire '13 Cards' team!', written in a playful, handwritten font, accompanied by a small, joyful character with curly hair and floating hearts, expressing profound gratitude. The scene then fades to black, bringing the credits to a close, with the piano melody gently fading out.
